mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing is sustained rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or the triggered hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum stays elevated while FEDFUNDS is already high, real-rate pressure remains restrictive and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as traders reprice policy persistence.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign does not turn negative in the crossed high-inflation/high-rate regime, or if 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations over a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects weakening household income growth and higher recession odds, which depresses cyclicals and broad market sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when unemployment rises quickly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment jumps of at least 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if Rank IC stays below 0.02 in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial utilization and broadening real activity, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when factory output is not simultaneously weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trends fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for rising INDPRO periods is not above 55% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST is also firm, the combination signals resilient consumption and housing-linked demand, which should support equity earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive demand signals do not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 58% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO paired with a rising UNRATE identifies an unfolding demand-supply slump, which compresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined downturn condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the signal loses explanatory power in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in HOUST signals stronger future construction activity, jobs, and capex spillovers, which should improve market breadth and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained HOUST uptrends are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship reverses during more than one 12-month rolling subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, stable PAYEMS with low UNRATE supports household income and reduces recession pricing, so equity returns stay positive over the next month; the same labor stability loses power once unemployment begins rising.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns do not cluster when PAYEMS is stable and UNRATE is flat or falling, or if the effect disappears entirely after controlling for the level of unemployment.
